summary: This bundle refreshes deterministic proof that legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel and should migrate to install.sh.

evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the docs/guides/cli.md claim that legacy Homebrew is not a supported update channel and should migrate to install.sh is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Cautilus can automatically migrate a live Homebrew installation
Homebrew formula metadata is inspected at runtime
Every user has already removed legacy Homebrew installs

notableSubtests: renderInstallerUrl points at the public install.sh path
runInstallSmoke drives the install.sh channel through an isolated workspace
parseArgs rejects legacy Homebrew as an install smoke channel
install.sh downloads a tagged binary asset and writes a wrapper that preserves caller context
package metadata does not claim an npm bin install surface

decision.reason: The release smoke runner accepts the install.sh channel, rejects legacy Homebrew as a smoke channel, the distribution-surface test keeps install.sh valid, and the current CLI guide plus maintainer docs direct legacy Homebrew users to uninstall and reinstall through install.sh.
